notes: LinkWorks discontinued after DEC-Compaq-HP mergers; replaced by SharePoint and other ECM platforms
WebWorker was web gateway for LinkWorks; discontinued with LinkWorks itself
CORBA widely adopted in late 1990s but superseded by web services (SOAP, REST) by 2005
COM evolved into .NET component model; ActiveX largely deprecated in modern browsers
Java became one of world's most-used programming languages; JavaBeans less dominant but Java EE/Jakarta EE active
OLE evolved into COM automation; largely superseded by .NET and web APIs
Distributed objects replaced by SOA/web services/microservices architectures
DMS evolved into Enterprise Content Management (ECM); SharePoint, Documentum, OpenText dominant
Workflow evolved into BPM (Business Process Management) platforms; now includes RPA and no-code
Groupware evolved into modern collaboration platforms; Slack, Teams, Google Workspace now dominant
Digital UNIX renamed Tru64 UNIX; Compaq then HP maintained; final support ended 2012
OpenVMS still maintained by VSI (VMS Software Inc.) on x86 as of 2026
BackOffice suite retired circa 2002; components evolved into separate Windows Server products
Web browser became dominant universal client; Internet Explorer then Chrome/Firefox dominant
RPCs still used in gRPC and other modern frameworks; largely replaced by REST for web services
